---
title: "Where the trails meet the sightings"
subtitle: "Overlaying my iNaturalist observations on my Strava running and hiking tracks"
description: >
  Two datasets, one map: every running and hiking track from Strava, with my
  iNaturalist sightings dropped on top. How much of what I find, I find on the move.
date: 2026-06-26
categories: [nature, running, trail, data, api, south africa]
image: crossover-map.png
image-alt: "iNaturalist sightings overlaid on Strava tracks"
freeze: false
draft: true
---

I keep two logbooks without meaning to. [Strava](https://www.strava.com) records
where I run and hike; [iNaturalist](https://www.inaturalist.org) records what I
find. They cover the same mountains above Cape Town, so the obvious question is:
**how much of what I observe, I observe on the move?** Both feeds are pulled from
their APIs, so this answers itself and stays current.

```{python}
#| label: load
#| echo: false
import json, math
import numpy as np
strava = json.load(open("../trail-running-strava/strava_runs.json"))["stats"]
inat = json.load(open("../inaturalist-sightings/inat.json"))
tracks = strava["tracks"]
coords = inat["coords"]

# Bound the analysis to where I actually move (track bounding box + margin).
tlat = [p[0] for t in tracks for p in t]
tlng = [p[1] for t in tracks for p in t]
margin = 0.01
lo_lat, hi_lat = min(tlat) - margin, max(tlat) + margin
lo_lng, hi_lng = min(tlng) - margin, max(tlng) + margin
inbox = [c for c in coords if lo_lat < c["lat"] < hi_lat and lo_lng < c["lng"] < hi_lng]

# Distance (m) from each sighting to the nearest track point (equirectangular).
pts = np.array([(p[0], p[1]) for t in tracks for p in t])
def nearest_m(c):
    dlat = (pts[:, 0] - c["lat"]) * 110540
    dlng = (pts[:, 1] - c["lng"]) * 111320 * math.cos(math.radians(c["lat"]))
    return float(np.hypot(dlat, dlng).min())
dist = np.array([nearest_m(c) for c in inbox])
on_trail = inbox if len(inbox) == 0 else [c for c, d in zip(inbox, dist) if d < 200]
gen = inat["generated_at"][:10]
```

```{python}
#| label: stat-cards
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
def card(v, l):
    return (f'<div style="flex:1;min-width:130px;border:1px solid #e2e2e2;border-radius:10px;'
            f'padding:.9rem 1rem;text-align:center"><div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;'
            f'color:#0b5e54">{v}</div><div style="font-size:.8rem;color:#666">{l}</div></div>')
pct200 = round(100 * (dist < 200).sum() / max(len(dist), 1))
species_on_trail = len({c["species"] for c in on_trail if c["species"]})
HTML('<div style="display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:1rem 0">' + "".join([
    card(f'{len(inbox):,}', "sightings in my running/hiking area"),
    card(f'{pct200}%', "within 200 m of a track"),
    card(f'{species_on_trail}', "species found along the routes"),
]) + '</div>')
```

## The map

The map below is interactive — pan, zoom, and click any dot for the species.
Faint grey lines are my Strava tracks; each coloured dot is an iNaturalist
sighting, coloured by taxonomic group. Threatened taxa are excluded —
iNaturalist obscures their locations, so they never appear here.

```{python}
#| label: fig-crossover-thumb
#| include: false
# Static version, hidden: it only writes crossover-map.png, the post's listing
# thumbnail. The on-page map below is the interactive (folium) one.
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
GROUP_COLORS = {
    "Plantae": "#2e7d32", "Aves": "#1565c0", "Insecta": "#ef6c00",
    "Mammalia": "#6d4c41", "Reptilia": "#7b1fa2", "Amphibia": "#00838f",
    "Fungi": "#ad1457", "Arachnida": "#c62828", "Mollusca": "#5d4037",
    "Actinopterygii": "#0097a7", "Animalia": "#455a64",
}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
for t in tracks:
    ax.plot([p[1] for p in t], [p[0] for p in t], color="#cfcfcf", lw=1, zorder=1)
seen = set()
for c in inbox:
    g = c["group"]
    ax.scatter(c["lng"], c["lat"], s=14, color=GROUP_COLORS.get(g, "#888"),
               alpha=0.8, edgecolors="none", zorder=2,
               label=g if g not in seen else None)
    seen.add(g)
ax.set_aspect(1 / math.cos(math.radians(np.mean(tlat))))
ax.set_xticks([]); ax.set_yticks([])
for sp in ax.spines.values():
    sp.set_visible(False)
ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=7, framealpha=0.9, markerscale=1.4)
fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig("crossover-map.png", dpi=130)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-interactive
#| echo: false
import folium

m = folium.Map(tiles="CartoDB positron", control_scale=True)

# Strava tracks: faint grey lines.
tracks_fg = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Strava tracks", show=True)
for t in tracks:
    folium.PolyLine([(p[0], p[1]) for p in t], color="#777",
                    weight=1.5, opacity=0.35).add_to(tracks_fg)
tracks_fg.add_to(m)

# iNaturalist sightings: circle markers coloured by taxonomic group.
sight_fg = folium.FeatureGroup(name="iNaturalist sightings", show=True)
for c in inbox:
    name = c.get("common_name") or c.get("species") or "Unknown"
    popup = folium.Popup(
        f"<b>{name}</b><br><i>{c.get('species', '')}</i><br>"
        f"<span style='color:#888'>{c['group']}</span>", max_width=220)
    folium.CircleMarker(
        [c["lat"], c["lng"]], radius=3.5, weight=0, fill=True,
        fill_color=GROUP_COLORS.get(c["group"], "#888"),
        fill_opacity=0.85, popup=popup).add_to(sight_fg)
sight_fg.add_to(m)

# Frame to the area my tracks cover, and add a layer toggle + colour legend.
m.fit_bounds([[lo_lat, lo_lng], [hi_lat, hi_lng]])
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m)

present = [g for g in GROUP_COLORS if any(c["group"] == g for c in inbox)]
legend = ("<div style='position:fixed;bottom:18px;left:18px;z-index:9999;"
          "background:rgba(255,255,255,.92);padding:8px 10px;border:1px solid #ccc;"
          "border-radius:6px;font-size:12px;line-height:1.5'>"
          + "".join(f"<div><span style='display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;"
                    f"border-radius:50%;background:{GROUP_COLORS[g]};"
                    f"margin-right:6px'></span>{g}</div>" for g in present)
          + "</div>")
m.get_root().html.add_child(folium.Element(legend))
m
```

## How close to the trail?

Most sightings aren't *on* a track — they're a short detour off it. The
distribution of distance-to-nearest-track shows how far I tend to wander:

```{python}
#| label: fig-distance
#| fig-cap: "Distance from each sighting to my nearest recorded track."
#| echo: false
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 2.8))
ax.hist(np.clip(dist, 0, 1000), bins=30, color="#0b5e54")
ax.axvline(200, color="#c0392b", ls="--", lw=1)
ax.set_xlabel("metres to nearest track"); ax.set_ylabel("sightings")
fig.tight_layout()
```

```{python}
#| label: by-group
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from collections import Counter
near = Counter(c["group"] for c in on_trail).most_common(5)
if near:
    line = ", ".join(f"**{g}** ({n})" for g, n in near)
    print(f"Within 200 m of a track, the groups I record most are: {line}.")
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How this works
This page reads the two committed data files — `strava_runs.json` and
`inat.json` — produced by the Strava and iNaturalist fetch jobs. The analysis is
bounded to the area my tracks cover, distances use an equirectangular
approximation (fine at city scale), and only iNaturalist's non-obscured
coordinates are used, so no threatened taxon is ever located.

```{python}
#| label: refreshed
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
print(f"*Data refreshed {gen}. Powered by Strava · observations via iNaturalist.*")
```
:::
